## [03:01 - 06:01]

So, kind of the crux of what we teach with ads, in the copy accelerator group is I've kind of termed it zebra ads, so most ads blend in, if you think of a zebra, it's pretty damn hard not to see a zebra, like a zebra runs by, you walk and done at the zoo, you see a zebra, your eyes go directly to that, it immediately gets attention. So, we kind of have a three step formula with this, which is very simple, it's grab their attention, hook them with something that's interesting or curiosity based, and then kind of sell the click. And the biggest thing though here is really the four kinds of zebra ads. So, Steph and I have written tons and tons of ads, we've helped people with tons of ads in our group, and over and over again we basically see there's kind of four frameworks that our go-tos, because they just work over and over again. So, we're gonna go through some of these, one of which is the emotional story ad, this is always our go-to, if you have a really good emotional story, whether that's on Facebook, whether that's in email, whether that's on a native ad that you're just putting two to three sentences in there, those tend to work really, really well, and when they hit, they tend to be the biggest home runs. We've seen that over and over again, and like I said, we're gonna go through examples of all these. The second one is kind of what I mentioned before, topical news stuff, something that is on your customer's mind that they're thinking about right now. So, we'll go through examples of that. Obviously stuff with the election and election years is huge, if you're in health survival, like coronavirus was absolutely something you should be tapping into right now, stuff like that that is always on people's minds. Third one is curiosity, and there's a lot of these that are just super curiosity based, where they really don't give away anything, it's super blind, but it just peaks your curiosity so much that you have to click the ad. The last one is a contrarian angle, so everybody's kind of going one way and saying one thing in your niche, and you kind of go the complete opposite way. Those tend to work really well.
